title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During an investigation into the murder of Miss Mulgrew, the superintendent informs the group that while only Miss Simmons is currently targeted by warnings, everyone has the right to legal counsel. Mrs. Swetnam provides a statement claiming she was darning socks at four o'clock, but later clarifies that she spent time cleaning a blocked gutter in the rain. She notes the rain began at 4:15, and she finished her task around 4:40. She has no witnesses to verify her being outside, though she points to the clean gutter as evidence. Edmund Swetnam states he was sleeping and did not hear his mother call him.

Mrs. Eastbrook initially claims she was in the study with Colonel Eastbrook listening to the radio, but the Colonel contradicts her, stating he was talking to a farmer named Mr. Lumpson from 3:45 until just before tea at 4:45. Mrs. Eastbrook then changes her story, admitting she went for a walk at 3:30. Chief Inspector Corrado explains that Miss McGarrett had identified a person she did not see in a room during a robbery. Miss Chilcote suggests Miss McGarrett saw everything from behind a door.

Mickey, the kitchen worker, interrupts the proceedings, claiming she has witnessed something important. She declares she is no longer willing to stay silent for money because she fears for her life. Mickey testifies that on the night of the robbery, she was in the dining room and saw Miss Blacklock holding a gun next to a man. Coladock interrupts, accusing Edmund of stealing Colonel Eastbrook's revolver and conspiring with Rudi Schlesinger. Coladock alleges that Edmund followed Patrick Simmons into the drawing room, shot Miss Blacklock, killed Rudi Schlesinger, and then returned to the room.
